channel.title: scverse x cellpainting 2026 on scverse x cellpainting

item.title: Accommodation & travel
item.description:
Staying central & commuting

Many attendees prefer to stay in central Berlin for the food, atmosphere, and choice of hotels, and commute to the venue. The campus sits on the S-Bahn S2 line, so the easiest commute is to book somewhere on or near the S2.

  • Look for hotels near S2 stations such as Friedrichstraße, Nordbahnhof, Oranienburger Straße, Brandenburger Tor, Potsdamer Platz, Gesundbrunnen, Bornholmer Straße, or Pankow — these give you a direct ride north toward Berlin-Buch.
  • Allow roughly 35–45 minutes door-to-door depending on where you stay, including the S2 ride and the short bus 353 connection at the end.
  • Plan exact routes and times with the BVG or VBB journey planner — search for the destination “Campus Buch”.

Getting to the campus

  • Take S-Bahn S2 (direction Bernau) to Berlin-Buch station, then bus 353 (direction “Campus Buch”) to the stop on campus. Buses 150, 158, and 259 also serve the area.
  • Berlin-Buch is in fare zone B, covered by a standard Berlin AB ticket. If you stay just over the border in Brandenburg (e.g. Panketal), the local stations are in zone C, so you’ll need an ABC ticket.
  • Full venue directions: How to get to Berlin-Buch (MDC). Campus address: Robert-Rössle-Straße 10, 13125 Berlin.

Questions?

Any questions about getting here or finding a place to stay? Just reply to your invitation email or reach out on the hackathon Zulip channel. We’re happy to help.
